ChartSampleFieldSet.description: Optionally select which set of fields are returned in the Data array. Currently only applicable for asset types (Warrants, MiniFutures and Certificates).

Count.description: Specifies maximum number of samples in the snapshot to return. Maximum is 1200. If not specified a default of 1200 samples are returned.

DataCurrency.description: Currency conversion in which data is expected in growth.

DataSeriesType.description: Growth or Price | Growth in case of mutual funds and Price in case of other asset classes like Fx.

FieldGroups.description: Use FieldGroups to add additional information to the snapshot samples like display/formatting details or information about the price source. If FieldGroups are not specified in the request then the response defaults to only hold the bare OHLC samples and nothing else.

Horizon.description: The time period that each sample covers. Values are interpreted in minutes: 1, 2, 3, 5, 10, 15, 30, 60, 120, 180, 240, 300, 360, 480, 1440, 10080, 43200, 129600, 518400.

ContextId.description: The streaming context id that this request is associated with. This parameter must only contain letters (a-z) and numbers (0-9) as well as - (dash) and _ (underscore). It is case insensitive. Max length is 50 characters.

Format.description: Optional Media type (RFC 2046) of the serialized data updates that are streamed to the client. Currently only application/json and application/x-protobuf is supported. If an unrecognized format is specified, the subscription end point will return HTTP status code 400 - Bad format.

ReferenceId.description: Mandatory client specified reference id for the subscription. This parameter must only contain alphanumberic characters as well as - (dash) and _ (underscore). Cannot start with _. It is case insensitive. Max length is 50 characters.

RefreshRate.description: Optional custom refresh rate, measured in milliseconds, between each data update. Note that it is not possible to get a refresh rate lower than the rate specified in the customer service level agreement (SLA).

ReplaceReferenceId.description: Reference id of the subscription that should be replaced.

Tag Obsolete.description: : Optional client specified tag used for grouping subscriptions.

EmailAddress.description: The email address to send alert notifications to.

NotifyWithMail.description: Send an email when a notification is triggered.

Amount.description: Value of periodic transfer

Amount.description: Amount charged to the account for this trade..

AmountInAccountCurrency.description: Amount charged to the account for this trade converted to account currency (if different from instrument currency).

ChargeTypeId.description: Unique ID of this charge type.

Currency.description: Currency of this charge (typically the same as instrument currency).

AccountKey.description: Unique key identifying the account to register the trade on.

AccruedInterest.description: Accrued interest charged to the buyer/from the seller. Only applies to Bond trades.

Amount.description: Specifies the size (in units or as cash amount) for an order or price request. Certain products can be traded in fractions.

AssetType.description: AssetType available for DealCapture currently supports Stock, Bond and MutualFund.

BuySell.description: Specifies the side of the trade.

Charges.description: List of charges associated to this trade.

Commission.description: Commission charged for this trade (in instrument currency).

CommissionInAccountCurrency.description: Commission charged for this trade in the currency of the account (if different from instrument currency).

ConversionRate.description: If the instrument currency is different from the account currency that this trade is posted to, this field indicates the conversion rate that was applied for this trade.

CounterBookingAccountKey Obsolete.description: The counter booking account for the Partner or Broker.

ExecutionTime.description: SO 8601 representation of datetime, always in UTC timezone (Z) with 3-decimal precision for milliseconds.

ExternalOrderId.description: A client-generated unique string of maximum 50 characters that can be used to match a trade to an external system order. This field is not validated for uniqueness and will not be rejected if the provided `ExternalOrderId` was passed in a previous request. Ensure that the provided value is generated using high-entropy randomness to prevent collisions (such as a `GUID` or `ULID`).

ExternalReference.description: A client-generated unique string that can be used to match an entity(such as an order) to an external system. Note: OpenAPI does not validate this field for uniqueness and will not reject operations if the provided ExternalReference was passed in a previous request. This field is used as 'tag' on the entity that allows the client to easily find this instance among equivalents. Ensure that the provided value is generated using high-entropy randomness to prevent collisions(such as a GUID or ULID).

IsAdvisedTrade.description: Indicates whether this trade was the result of a advisory order that was confirmed by the end-client.

MarketCounterPartyId.description: CounterPartyId that this trade was performed against.

Price.description: Specifies the price level for an order. Can also be used in a price request to indicate the target price level for calculation of costs.

TraderId.description: Specifies the ID of the trader that performed the trade (on behalf of the end client).

TradingVenue.description: Specifies the Trade Venue that the trade was performed on.

Uic.description: Integer representing a universal identifier that is used to mark a specific/unique Instrument. Can be used in combination with AssetType to uniquely identify an Instrument.

ValueDate.description: Value date of the trade formatted as YYYY-MM-DD. Must be a trade day (cannot be weekend/holiday).

AcceptRemainderAmount.description: Specifies if an account can accept the remaining units, if applicable.

AccountId.description: AccountId of account, which is part of allocation. When creating allocation key, then either AccountKey or AccountId is required.

AccountKey.description: AccountKey of account, which is part of allocation. When creating allocation key, then either AccountKey or AccountId is required.

Priority.description: If several accounts are defined with AcceptRemainderAmount=true, the priority determines the priority by which remainders are distributed between these accounts Also when allocating partial fills, it is allocated in the prioritized order

UnitValue.description: The actual amount or percentage of the total trade to be distributed to this account, see AllocationUnitType for further explanation.

AllocationUnitType.description: Specifies how to interpret the value of UnitValue in the AllocationKeyAccountInfo.

MarginHandling.description: Represents the behavior if because of margin reason entire order can not be processed.

OneTime.description: Whether the key is for one time use.

OwnerAccountKey.description: Account key for the account to this allocation key belongs to.

StrikeStartIndex.description: The starting index of the strikes to return The number of strikes counted from this is determined by the MaxNumberOfStrikes of the request

AccountKey.description: Unique key identifying the account used in retrieving the price. Only required when calling context represents an authenticated user.

Amount.description: Order size, defaults to minimal order size for given instrument.

Expiries.description: Expiries are a collection of expiries requested and the window of strike-rows for each expiry. This allows us to ask explicitly for a certain selection of the total set of option chain data. If not provided the service picks the 3 first expiries and assume a centered start-index for all expiries. Asking for data for an expiry that does not exist or a start index higher than the number of strike rows available for that expiry, is not exactly an error and I believe it should just lead to no data being returned.

Identifier.description: The instrument's identifier. Use UIC for FX. For Contract Options use group root id or option root id - both will return all options within specified group root or in case of option root id - all options that are in the same group as specified option root.

MaxStrikesPerExpiry.description: Signals a request to only get N strike-rows per expiry. Default is 12. If no explicit StartIndex is provided, the N rows are assumed to be centered on the strike closes to the mid-spot price. I.e. it could be for an expiry with 16 strike-rows, a request to show 12 strikes-per-expiry means that the start-index for that expiry will be 2, leading the strike-rows to be returned to be the [2-13] or [2-14[ range.

ContextId.description: The context id part of the streaming session (used to identify the subscription within a streaming session).

DurationType.description: Order duration type. Defines the time period during which an order is valid. If the period expires (for instance for a DayOrder or GoodTillDate order), the order is cancelled automatically.

ExpirationDateContainsTime.description: Indicates whether the ExpirationDateTime field contains the time alongside a date. Only applicable for GoodTillDate OrderDurationType.

ExpirationDateTime.description: Expiration date and (optionally) time if the order duration type is GoodTillDate. If no time is provided, orders are cancelled at the end of the trading day during overnight processing. Note: the time value of this field (if provided) is always expressed in the timezone of the exchange on which the instrument is traded. The time value only supports hours and minutes formatted as HH:MM. For instance, a 15:00 expiration time for an instrument traded on NYSE will be cancelled automatically at 3pm local time (NY) if the order is not triggered before this time.

For withdrawal transactions this field will hold the AccountKey of the clients trading account. Remember that the currency of the funding account and the clients trading account must be the same.

FundingCheck.description: Specify this to specify if funding rules be checked and enforced. Default is "Enforce". Enforce: All margin and funding checks will be performed and enforced.The transaction will fail if margin and funding checks are not passed. Ignore: No margin or funding checks are performed.The operation will move the specified monies from the client account and you can ultimately place the clients accounts into negative. You can only specify FundingCheck:Ignore for a cash withdrawal from a clients trading account back to the WLC's funding account. If you specify FundingCheck:Ignore for a funding from the WLC's funding account to the clients trading account, the call will fail.

ToAccountKey.description: Account where the cash is moved to. For funding transactions, this field will hold the AccountKey of the clients trading account. For withdrawal transactions this field will hold the AccountKey of the WLCs funding account. Remember that the currency of the funding account and the clients trading account must be the same.

InstrumentDelta.description: Delta measures the degree to which an option is exposed to shifts in the price of the underlying instrument. A one-point change in the underlying instrument's price affects this option's value by the Delta amount.

InstrumentGamma.description: Gamma measures the rate of change of an option's price with fluctuations in the price of the underlying instrument. A one-point change in the underlying instrument's price changes this option's Delta by the Gamma amount.

InstrumentTheta.description: Theta measures the rate of decline in the value of an option due to the passage of time and is also known as 'time decay'. The value of the option changes by the Theta amount for each passing day.

InstrumentThetaInAccountCurrency.description: InstrumentTheta of the instrument in account currency.

InstrumentVega.description: Vega measures the rate of change between an option's value and the underlying asset's implied volatility. A one percentage point increase in implied volatility shifts the option's bid/ask price by the Vega amount.

MidVol.description: The mid rate for the implied volatility used in pricing this option

PositionThetaInAccountCurrency.description: InstrumentTheta of the position in account currency.

Theta.description: Refers to the combined time decay of all the options in a particular trading position. I.e. the sum of the individual theta values for each option.

ThetaCurrency.description: The currency of the Theta value.

ThetaDisplayDecimals.description: The number of decimal places in the Theta value. Used for display purposes.

Vega.description: Sensitivity of a position with respect to the implied volatility used to price FX Options. This shows how much money is made (positive number) or lost (negative number) when volatility goes up by one percentage point.

VegaCurrency.description: The currency of the Vega value.
